headline: The Average American Has Worked for 6 Employers and 23% Still Have Multiple Old Retirement Accounts, Fidelity’s 2026 Study Finds
summary: The typical American worker now passes through six employers over a career, and each job change leaves behind a retirement account to keep track of. Fidelity’s 2026 study found that 23% of Americans with retirement accounts still have multiple balances sitting in old or current workplace plans. That fragmentation is the quiet drag on retirement ... The Average American Has Worked for 6 Employers and 23% Still Have Multiple Old Retirement Accounts, Fidelity’s 2026 Study Finds
